title: Paris coup d'état and massacres of 1418 - Wikipedia
highlights:
On 12–13 June, a mob attacked the prisons where the Armagnacs and their supporters were held. Between one and two thousand people were killed. Among the dead were a constable of France, a chancellor of France, four bishops and several university professors. On 21 August, there was another lesser massacre of Armagnac prisoners.  ... title: Holy Innocents’ Cemetery: Mass Grave in Medieval Paris before the Catacombs
highlights:
A mass grave since 1223, bodies were exhumed and the bones were moved to the Catacombs in 1786.

---  ...    The Holy Innocents’ Cemetery (French: Cimetière des Saints-Innocents or Cimetière des Innocents) is a defunct cemetery in Paris that was used from the Middle Ages until the late 18th century. It was the oldest and largest cemetery in Paris and had often been used for mass graves.[1] It was closed because of overuse in 1780, and in 1786 the remaining corpses were exhumed and transported to the unused subterranean quarries near Montparnasse known as the Catacombs.
